# Task: Restructure `coach` into a pnpm monorepo (pipeline package + web app), deployable to Vercel as a static SPA

  You are working in the `coach` repo. Read `CLAUDE.md` first and obey its workflow rules (branch + PR, never commit to `main`; `pnpm check` must pass; quality gates are enforced by hooks — fix code, don't weaken
  configs).

  ## Why

  Today `coach` is a single package: pure data-processing in `src/etl` + `src/graph`, a React/React-Flow visualization in `viz/` (built to a single HTML file via `vite-plugin-singlefile`), and Node glue scripts in
  `scripts/` that read files from disk, run the pipeline, inject the resulting view model into the prebuilt HTML, and write `out/*.html`.

  I want to ship this as a **static web app on Vercel (no server)** where:

  1. A user lands on a clean page with an **Upload** button.
  2. They upload **one or more native Claude Code log files** (`.jsonl`), **or** a **log + OTEL traces** set (`logs.json` + `trace.json` / `trace-*.json`).
  3. The upload is **processed entirely in the browser** to produce the "digested" view-model data, which is then **rendered as the graph**.

  This is step one of cleanly **separating data processing from visualization**: the upload triggers processing; the visualization consumes only the digested data. I want this seam designed so that **later I can
  replace the in-browser processing call with a single HTTP API call to a backend** without touching the visualization.

  ## Current architecture (verify by reading these before changing anything)

  - `src/etl/` — **pure, no `node:*` imports** (confirmed). Key exports:
    - `nativeSessionToTrace(jsonl: string): TempoTrace` (`native.ts`)
    - `enrichTrace(trace, logs): TempoTrace` (`enrich.ts`)
    - `transformTrace(trace): TraceNode[]` (`transform.ts`)
    - `addSessionNode`, `aggregateSession`, `groupSessionsByAgent`, `aggregateAgent` (`aggregate.ts`)
    - types in `types.ts`, zod schema in `tempo.schema.ts`
  - `src/graph/view-model.ts` — pure. Exports `buildCausalGraphView`, `buildSessionCausalGraphView`, `buildAgentCausalGraphView` and the view types `CausalGraphView`, `SessionCausalGraphView`,
  `AgentCausalGraphView`.
  - `src/fixtures/` — `native-claude/*` (`.jsonl`) and `otel/*` (`trace.json`/`trace-*.json` + `logs.json`); used by tests **and** by the e2e script.
  - `scripts/e2e.ts` — **this is the reference for mode selection**: native vs single-trace vs multi-trace session vs multi-session-by-`user_id`, and which view (`interaction` / `session` / `agent`) to build. It
  constructs `VizData = { kind: 'agent'|'session'|'interaction', data }` and injects it into `viz/dist/index.html` as `window.__TRACE_DATA__`.
  - `scripts/viz.ts` — builds HTML from a saved `nodes*.json`.
  - `viz/` — Vite + React + `@xyflow/react` SPA. `App.tsx` reads `window.__TRACE_DATA__` / `window.__TRACE_TITLE__`. **`viz/src/types.ts` duplicates the view-model types** (`GraphViewNode`, `CausalGraphView`,
  `VizData`, etc.).

  The whole pipeline already takes strings/objects in and returns plain data — only `scripts/` touch the filesystem. Exploit that.

  ## Target structure

  Convert to a pnpm-workspace monorepo with two packages plus the existing scripts:

  ```
  packages/
    pipeline/                 # was src/  — pure, runs in BOTH browser and node
      src/
        etl/                  # moved as-is (with co-located *.test.ts)
        graph/                # moved as-is (with co-located *.test.ts)
        orchestrate.ts        # NEW — see below
        index.ts              # public exports: pipeline fns + orchestrate + ALL view/VizData types
      fixtures/               # moved from src/fixtures (update test + script paths)
      package.json            # name: "@coach/pipeline", type: module, exports map
      tsconfig.json
    app/                      # was viz/  — the web application, contains the visualization
      src/
        viz/                  # App.tsx, layout.ts, TraceNode.tsx (moved from viz/src)
        upload/               # NEW landing page + file intake UI
        data-source.ts        # NEW seam: processUploads(files) -> Promise<VizData>
        main.tsx
      index.html
      vite.config.ts
      package.json            # name: "@coach/app", depends on "@coach/pipeline"
      tsconfig.json
  scripts/                    # node CLI — rewired to import @coach/pipeline orchestrator
  ```

  Update `pnpm-workspace.yaml` to include `packages/*`. Keep root `package.json` as the workspace root that runs `pnpm check` across all packages.

  ## Required changes

  ### 1. Pipeline package (`@coach/pipeline`)
  - Move `src/etl`, `src/graph`, `src/fixtures` and their tests; fix all import/fixture paths so `pnpm test` passes.
  - **Single source of truth for view types:** export `GraphViewNode`, `GraphViewEdge`, `GraphViewThread`, `CausalGraphView`, `SessionCausalGraphView`, `AgentCausalGraphView`, and a `VizData` union from the
  pipeline's `index.ts`. Delete `viz/src/types.ts`'s duplicates and have the app import them from `@coach/pipeline`.
  - **New `orchestrate.ts`** — the file-system-free brain currently embedded in `scripts/e2e.ts`. Design an in-memory input model, e.g.:
    ```ts
    export interface UploadedFile { name: string; content: string }
    export interface VizResult { title: string; data: VizData }
    export function buildVizResults(files: readonly UploadedFile[]): VizResult[]
    ```
    It must reproduce `e2e.ts`'s semantics on a flat list of in-memory files instead of directory walking:
    - any `*.jsonl` → native: `nativeSessionToTrace → transformTrace → addSessionNode` → pick view
    - `logs.json` + single `trace.json` → `enrichTrace → transformTrace → addSessionNode`
    - `logs.json` + multiple `trace-*.json` → per-trace then `aggregateSession`, plus an agent view via `aggregateAgent`
    - view selection identical to today (agent if available, else session, else interaction)
    Keep the grouping rules **explicit and documented in code**. v1 must cover: (a) one or more native `.jsonl` uploads, (b) one OTEL set (`logs.json` + one or more `trace*.json`). Multi-session-by-`user_id` from a
  flat browser upload can be a documented stretch/future item (optionally support folder upload via `webkitdirectory`); do not regress the CLI's multi-session behavior.
  - Must remain **import-clean for the browser**: no `node:*`, no `fs`. Keep zod.

  ### 2. App package (`@coach/app`)
  - Move the `viz/` React app in. Replace the `window.__TRACE_DATA__` global-injection mechanism with a normal data flow:
    - **Landing page** with a clear Upload control (drag-drop + file picker; accept `.jsonl` and `.json`).
    - On upload, read files in the browser (`File.text()`), build `UploadedFile[]`, and call the **data-source seam**.
    - **`data-source.ts`** exposes `processUploads(files: UploadedFile[]): Promise<VizResult[]>`. The **default implementation calls `@coach/pipeline` `buildVizResults` locally**. Document clearly (comment +
  ARCHITECTURE.md) that this is the single function to later swap for `fetch('/api/process', …)` against a backend — the visualization must depend only on `VizResult`/`VizData`, never on pipeline internals.
  panel, minimap).
  - Vite SPA that builds to **static assets** (drop `vite-plugin-singlefile`; normal multi-file static build is correct for Vercel). `pnpm --filter @coach/app dev` must serve the upload landing page.

  ### 3. Scripts / CLI
  - Rewire `scripts/e2e.ts` and `scripts/viz.ts` to read files from disk (Node) and delegate mode logic to `@coach/pipeline`'s `buildVizResults` — no duplicated orchestration logic. CLI output (`out/*`) behavior
  should remain equivalent. If injecting into a prebuilt HTML no longer fits the static-app model, it's fine to have the CLI emit `*.json` view-model artifacts and/or a thin standalone HTML; preserve the ability to
  eyeball fixture outputs. Keep the relevant `package.json` scripts working.

  ### 4. Tooling / quality gates (keep all green)
  - pnpm workspaces; `pnpm check` at root must run typecheck + lint + format:check + test + knip across packages and pass.
  - ESLint stays `strict-type-checked`, **warnings = errors**; Prettier; **Knip** must pass — reconfigure `knip.json` for the new multi-package layout (it currently just ignores `viz/**`); the React app likely
  needs Knip's vite/react awareness. Husky `pre-commit` + the `.claude` PostToolUse lint hook must still work.
  - Don't introduce `any`, don't relax `tsconfig` strictness, don't add eslint-disable to paper over issues.

  ### 5. Vercel
  - Make the app deployable as a **static site** on Vercel from this monorepo. Add minimal config (e.g. `vercel.json` and/or documented Project settings: root directory `packages/app`, build `pnpm --filter
  @coach/app build`, output `packages/app/dist`, install at repo root). **No serverless functions / no backend** in this change.

  ### 6. Documentation (important — this must not rot)
  - Create **`ARCHITECTURE.md`** at repo root and **reference it from `README.md`**. It must describe:
    - High-level architecture and the **processing ↔ visualization separation**.
    - The **package layout** and what belongs where (`@coach/pipeline` = pure data processing incl. orchestration; `@coach/app` = visualization + upload UI + data-source seam; `scripts/` = Node CLI over the same
  pipeline).
    - The **data flow**: inputs (native `.jsonl` / OTEL `logs.json` + `trace*.json`) → ETL → aggregate → view model → `VizData` → render.
    - The **upload flow** in the browser and the **`data-source.ts` seam** as the future swap point for an HTTP backend.
  - Update `README.md`'s existing **Pipeline diagram + the fixture/mode table** so they stay accurate after the restructure, and point readers to `ARCHITECTURE.md` for the full picture. (The README pipeline section
  and ARCHITECTURE.md must agree.)
  - Add a short note (in `CLAUDE.md` or at the top of `ARCHITECTURE.md`) that **`ARCHITECTURE.md` is a living document — update it in the same change whenever package layout, module boundaries, or data flow
  change.**

  ## Acceptance criteria
  - `pnpm install && pnpm check` passes at the repo root (typecheck, lint with `--max-warnings=0`, format:check, all vitest tests, knip) across both packages.
  - `@coach/pipeline` has **zero `node:*`/`fs` imports** and bundles cleanly for the browser.
  - `pnpm --filter @coach/app dev` shows a clean landing page with an Upload button; uploading the repo's native `.jsonl` fixture and the OTEL fixture set each renders the expected graph, processed entirely
  client-side.
  - View/`VizData` types are defined once in `@coach/pipeline` and imported by the app (no duplicate type files).
  - `scripts/e2e.ts` / `scripts/viz.ts` still produce fixture outputs using the shared orchestrator (no duplicated mode logic).
  - `packages/app` produces a static build deployable to Vercel; Vercel config/settings documented.
  - `ARCHITECTURE.md` exists, is referenced from `README.md`, and accurately reflects the final structure; README pipeline diagram/table updated.

  ## Out of scope
  Backend/server, auth, persistence, the eventual HTTP API implementation, and closing the feedback loop back to the agent. Just leave the `data-source.ts` seam clean for it.

  ## Process
  Work on a branch, open a PR, ensure `pnpm check` is green, and summarize the new structure and how to run/deploy the app in the PR description.

## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:
1. Use ToolSearch with `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__<tool_name>` to load the specific tool
2. Then call the tool

For example, to get tab context:
1. First: ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp"
2. Then: Call mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp

## grafana
This server provides access to your Grafana instance and the surrounding ecosystem.

Available Capabilities:
- Search: Find dashboards, folders, and other Grafana resources.
- Datasources: List and fetch details for datasources.
- Incidents: Search, create, update, and resolve incidents in Grafana Incident.
- Prometheus: Run PromQL queries, retrieve metric metadata, and explore label names/values.
- Loki: Run LogQL queries, retrieve log metadata, and explore label names/values.
- Alerting: List and fetch alert rules and notification contact points.
- Dashboards: Search, retrieve, update, and create dashboards. Extract panel queries and datasource information.
- Folders: Manage dashboard folders.
- OnCall: View and manage on-call schedules, shifts, teams, and users.
- Asserts: Query and analyze assertion data.
- Sift Investigations: Start and manage Sift investigations, analyze logs/traces, find error patterns, and detect slow requests.
- Pyroscope: Profile applications and fetch profiling data.
- Navigation: Generate deeplink URLs for Grafana resources like dashboards, panels, and Explore queries.
- Annotations: Create and manage dashboard annotations.
- Rendering: Export dashboard panels or full dashboards as PNG images (requires Grafana Image Renderer plugin).
- Plugins: Check whether Grafana plugins are installed and fetch plugin details.
- API: Make authenticated HTTP requests to any Grafana API endpoint with optional jq-style response filtering.
- Proxied Tools: Access tools from external MCP servers (like Tempo) through dynamic discovery.

Timestamp parameters without a timezone offset are interpreted as UTC. Include an offset like '-05:00' or use relative syntax like 'now-1h' to query in a different timezone.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

{
  "name": "coach",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Self-reflective feedback for agent harnesses, derived from OTEL trace analysis.",
  "type": "module",
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.33.0",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.11"
  },
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts --outDir dist",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "lint": "eslint . --max-warnings=0",
    "lint:fix": "eslint . --fix --max-warnings=0",
    "format": "prettier --write .",
    "format:check": "prettier --check .",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "enrich": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/enrich.ts",
    "etl": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/etl.ts",
    "e2e": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/e2e.ts",
    "viz": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/viz.ts",
    "viz:build": "pnpm --filter coach-viz build",
    "knip": "knip",
    "check": "pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm format:check && pnpm test && pnpm knip",
    "prepare": "husky"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{ts,tsx}": [
      "eslint --fix --max-warnings=0 --no-warn-ignored",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "*.{js,cjs,mjs}": [
      "eslint --fix --max-warnings=0 --no-warn-ignored",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "*.{json,md,yml,yaml}": [
      "prettier --write"
    ]
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.17.0",
    "@types/node": "^22.10.0",
    "eslint": "^9.17.0",
    "eslint-config-prettier": "^9.1.0",
    "husky": "^9.1.0",
    "knip": "^6.14.2",
    "lint-staged": "^15.2.0",
    "prettier": "^3.4.0",
    "tsup": "^8.5.1",
    "typescript": "^5.7.0",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.18.0",
    "vitest": "^2.1.0",
    "zod": "^4.4.3"
  },
  "pnpm": {
    "onlyBuiltDependencies": [
      "esbuild"
    ]
  }
}

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  # Runs when a PR is opened and on every push to that PR's branch
  # (opened, synchronize, reopened are the default pull_request activity types).
  pull_request:
  # Runs whenever main changes.
  push:
    branches: [main]

# Cancel superseded runs on the same ref to save CI minutes.
concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 24
          cache: pnpm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Typecheck
        run: pnpm typecheck

      - name: Lint
        run: pnpm lint

      - name: Format check
        run: pnpm format:check

      - name: Test
        run: pnpm test

      - name: Knip
        run: pnpm knip

content.content:
1	# coach
2	
3	Improve agent harnesses — accuracy, latency, cost, and detection of hallucinations and operational
4	errors — through **OpenTelemetry (OTEL) trace analysis**. OTEL keeps coach **harness-agnostic**.
5	
6	Unlike tracing built for engineers to observe agents, coach aims to reflect findings back to the
7	**agent itself**, with the engineer monitoring that loop. Stage one targets the engineer until we
8	learn which problems are solvable.
9	
10	## Pipeline
11	
12	```
13	trace.json / logs.json
14	        │
15	        ▼ ETL (per trace)
16	   enrichTrace → transformTrace → addSessionNode
17	        │
18	        ▼ Aggregation
19	   aggregateSession (per session)
20	   groupSessionsByAgent → aggregateAgent (per user_id)
21	        │
22	        ▼ View model (bottom-up)
23	   buildCausalGraphView        → CausalGraphView
24	   buildSessionCausalGraphView → SessionCausalGraphView
25	   buildAgentCausalGraphView   → AgentCausalGraphView
26	        │
27	        ▼ HTML
28	   buildCausalHtml / buildSessionCausalHtml / buildAgentCausalHtml
29	```
30	
31	View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by HTML renderers — no raw `TraceNode[]` reaches the rendering layer.
32	
33	### Fixture modes
34	
35	`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under `src/fixtures/`.
36	
37	| Input shape                        | Mode                | HTML outputs                                            |
38	| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
39	| `<dir>/trace.json` + `logs.json`   | Single trace        | `composition`, `causal`                                 |
40	| `<dir>/trace-*.json` + `logs.json` | Multi-trace session | above + `causal-session`, `causal-agent`                |
41	| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | per-session outputs + `causal-agent-{userId}` per agent |
42	
43	In multi-session mode the `user_id` embedded in each trace determines the agent — one HTML is produced per unique `user_id` across all sessions.
44	
45	## Quick start
46	
47	```bash
48	pnpm install
49	pnpm check     # typecheck + lint + format + test (same as CI)
50	```
51	
52	## Development
53	
54	| Command           | What it does                             |
55	| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
56	| `pnpm check`      | Full gate: typecheck, lint, format, test |
57	| `pnpm lint:fix`   | Auto-fix lint issues                     |
58	| `pnpm format`     | Auto-format with Prettier                |
59	| `pnpm test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode                     |
60	| `pnpm build`      | Emit `dist/`                             |
61	
62	Quality is enforced deterministically by committed hooks — a git `pre-commit` hook (Husky +
63	lint-staged), a Claude Code `PostToolUse` hook, and GitHub Actions CI. See `CLAUDE.md` for details.
64	
65	## Contributing workflow
66	
67	- Branch off `main`; **never commit to `main` directly**.
68	- Open a PR — CI runs on open, on every push to the branch, and on pushes to `main`.
69	
70	### Recommended one-time setup (requires a GitHub remote)
71	
72	Make CI a required check so PRs can't merge red:
73	
74	```bash
75	gh api -X PUT repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/main/protection \
76	  -F required_status_checks.strict=true \
77	  -F 'required_status_checks.contexts[]=check' \
78	  -F enforce_admins=true \
79	  -F required_pull_request_reviews.required_approving_review_count=1 \
80	  -F restrictions=
81	```
82

file.content:
# coach

Improve agent harnesses — accuracy, latency, cost, and detection of hallucinations and operational
errors — through **OpenTelemetry (OTEL) trace analysis**. OTEL keeps coach **harness-agnostic**.

Unlike tracing built for engineers to observe agents, coach aims to reflect findings back to the
**agent itself**, with the engineer monitoring that loop. Stage one targets the engineer until we
learn which problems are solvable.

## Pipeline

```
trace.json / logs.json
        │
        ▼ ETL (per trace)
   enrichTrace → transformTrace → addSessionNode
        │
        ▼ Aggregation
   aggregateSession (per session)
   groupSessionsByAgent → aggregateAgent (per user_id)
        │
        ▼ View model (bottom-up)
   buildCausalGraphView        → CausalGraphView
   buildSessionCausalGraphView → SessionCausalGraphView
   buildAgentCausalGraphView   → AgentCausalGraphView
        │
        ▼ HTML
   buildCausalHtml / buildSessionCausalHtml / buildAgentCausalHtml
```

View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by HTML renderers — no raw `TraceNode[]` reaches the rendering layer.

### Fixture modes

`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under `src/fixtures/`.

| Input shape                        | Mode                | HTML outputs                                            |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `<dir>/trace.json` + `logs.json`   | Single trace        | `composition`, `causal`                                 |
| `<dir>/trace-*.json` + `logs.json` | Multi-trace session | above + `causal-session`, `causal-agent`                |
| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | per-session outputs + `causal-agent-{userId}` per agent |

In multi-session mode the `user_id` embedded in each trace determines the agent — one HTML is produced per unique `user_id` across all sessions.

## Quick start

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm check     # typecheck + lint + format + test (same as CI)
```

## Development

| Command           | What it does                             |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `pnpm check`      | Full gate: typecheck, lint, format, test |
| `pnpm lint:fix`   | Auto-fix lint issues                     |
| `pnpm format`     | Auto-format with Prettier                |
| `pnpm test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode                     |
| `pnpm build`      | Emit `dist/`                             |

Quality is enforced deterministically by committed hooks — a git `pre-commit` hook (Husky +
lint-staged), a Claude Code `PostToolUse` hook, and GitHub Actions CI. See `CLAUDE.md` for details.

## Contributing workflow

- Branch off `main`; **never commit to `main` directly**.
- Open a PR — CI runs on open, on every push to the branch, and on pushes to `main`.

### Recommended one-time setup (requires a GitHub remote)

Make CI a required check so PRs can't merge red:

```bash
gh api -X PUT repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/main/protection \
  -F required_status_checks.strict=true \
  -F 'required_status_checks.contexts[]=check' \

{
  "name": "coach",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "private": true,
  "description": "Self-reflective feedback for agent harnesses, derived from OTEL trace analysis.",
  "type": "module",
  "packageManager": "pnpm@10.33.0",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.11"
  },
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsup src/index.ts --format esm --dts --outDir dist",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "lint": "eslint . --max-warnings=0",
    "lint:fix": "eslint . --fix --max-warnings=0",
    "format": "prettier --write .",
    "format:check": "prettier --check .",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "enrich": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/enrich.ts",
    "etl": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/etl.ts",
    "e2e": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/e2e.ts",
    "viz": "node --experimental-strip-types scripts/viz.ts",
    "viz:build": "pnpm --filter coach-viz build",
    "knip": "knip",
    "check": "pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm format:check && pnpm test && pnpm knip",
    "prepare": "husky"
  },
  "lint-staged": {
    "*.{ts,tsx}": [
      "eslint --fix --max-warnings=0 --no-warn-ignored",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "*.{js,cjs,mjs}": [
      "eslint --fix --max-warnings=0 --no-warn-ignored",
      "prettier --write"
    ],
    "*.{json,md,yml,yaml}": [
      "prettier --write"
    ]
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.17.0",
    "@types/node": "^22.10.0",
    "eslint": "^9.17.0",
    "eslint-config-prettier": "^9.1.0",
    "husky": "^9.1.0",
    "knip": "^6.14.2",
    "lint-staged": "^15.2.0",
    "prettier": "^3.4.0",
    "tsup": "^8.5.1",
    "typescript": "^5.7.0",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.18.0",
    "vitest": "^2.1.0",
    "zod": "^4.4.3"
  },
  "pnpm": {
    "onlyBuiltDependencies": [
      "esbuild"
    ]
  }
}

content.text: Two categories of issues: `TraceRFNodeData` needs an index signature to satisfy React Flow's `Node<T extends Record<string,unknown>>` constraint, and `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` catches `thread.members[i]` being possibly undefined. Let me read the formatted App.tsx first:

input.content:
> **Living document** — update this file in the same change whenever package layout,
> module boundaries, or data flow change. Keep it honest on structural changes; consult
> it before architectural tasks.

# Coach — Architecture

## Overview

Coach processes agent execution traces and renders them as an interactive causal graph.
The core thesis: harness-agnostic OTEL traces feed a pure data pipeline whose output can
be reflected back to the agent (or its engineer) for improvement.

The system is split into two packages plus a Node CLI layer:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  @coach/app  (packages/app)                             │
│  React SPA · Upload UI · Graph renderer                 │
│                                                         │
│  upload/UploadPage.tsx ──► data-source.ts ──► viz/App   │
│                               │                         │
│           ┌───────────────────┘                         │
│           ▼                                             │
│  @coach/pipeline (packages/pipeline)                    │
│  Pure data processing · No node:* imports               │
│  ETL → Aggregate → View model → VizData                 │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

scripts/          Node CLI — reads from disk, writes JSON artifacts
```

## Package layout

| Package / dir       | Purpose                                                       |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `packages/pipeline` | Pure data pipeline: ETL, aggregation, view-model, orchestration. Zero `node:*` imports — runs in browser and Node alike. |
| `packages/app`      | React SPA: upload landing page, graph visualization, data-source seam. |
| `scripts/`          | Node CLI over the same pipeline. Reads fixture files from disk, writes `out/*.json` artifacts. |

## Data flow

```
Input files
  *.jsonl                 native Claude Code session logs
  logs.json + trace*.json OTEL Tempo traces + structured log entries
        │
        ▼  packages/pipeline/src/etl/
   nativeSessionToTrace()    (*.jsonl → TempoTrace)
   enrichTrace()             (TempoTrace + LogEntry[] → enriched TempoTrace)
   transformTrace()          (TempoTrace → TraceNode[])
   addSessionNode()          (synthesise session node)
        │
        ▼  packages/pipeline/src/etl/aggregate.ts
   aggregateSession()        (merge multi-trace → session forest)
   aggregateAgent()          (add agent root node)
        │
        ▼  packages/pipeline/src/graph/view-model.ts
   buildCausalGraphView()        → CausalGraphView
   buildSessionCausalGraphView() → SessionCausalGraphView
   buildAgentCausalGraphView()   → AgentCausalGraphView
        │
        ▼  VizData  { kind: 'agent'|'session'|'interaction', data }
        │
        ▼  packages/app/src/viz/App.tsx  (React Flow graph renderer)
```

View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by the renderer — no raw `TraceNode[]`
reaches the visualization layer.

## Upload flow and the data-source seam

```
Browser
  UploadPage.tsx
    └── File.text() × N → UploadedFile[]
          └── data-source.ts :: processUploads(files)
                └── @coach/pipeline :: buildVizResults(files)   ← CURRENT
                        └── ETL + aggregate + view model
                              └── VizResult[]
                                    └── App.tsx renders graph
```

**`packages/app/src/data-source.ts` is the single swap point** for moving processing to a
backend. Replace its body with a `fetch('/api/process', ...)` call and nothing else in the
app changes — the visualization layer depends only on `VizResult` / `VizData`.

## Fixture modes

`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under
`packages/pipeline/fixtures/`.

| Input shape                        | Mode                | Artifacts produced                        |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `<dir>/*.jsonl`                    | Native session      | `vizdata-<name>.json` per file            |
| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace.json`   | Single OTEL trace   | `vizdata-trace.json`                      |
| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace-*.json` | Multi-trace session | per-trace + `vizdata-session.json` + `vizdata-agent.json` |
| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | all of the above, grouped by dir          |

Multi-session-by-`user_id` is supported by the CLI (directory walking). Flat browser
upload of multi-session data is a future item; consider `<input webkitdirectory>`.

## Deploying to Vercel (static SPA)

The app builds to static assets — no serverless functions.

```
Root directory:  packages/app
Build command:   pnpm --filter @coach/app build
Output dir:      packages/app/dist
Install command: pnpm install (at repo root)
```

See `vercel.json` for the committed configuration.

input.content:
# coach

Improve agent harnesses — accuracy, latency, cost, and detection of hallucinations and operational
errors — through **OpenTelemetry (OTEL) trace analysis**. OTEL keeps coach **harness-agnostic**.

Unlike tracing built for engineers to observe agents, coach aims to reflect findings back to the
**agent itself**, with the engineer monitoring that loop. Stage one targets the engineer until we
learn which problems are solvable.

See **[ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)** for the full picture: package layout, data flow, upload
seam, and Vercel deployment.

## Pipeline

```
*.jsonl / logs.json + trace*.json
        │
        ▼ ETL  (@coach/pipeline)
   nativeSessionToTrace / enrichTrace → transformTrace → addSessionNode
        │
        ▼ Aggregation
   aggregateSession  ·  groupSessionsByAgent → aggregateAgent
        │
        ▼ View model
   buildCausalGraphView        → CausalGraphView
   buildSessionCausalGraphView → SessionCausalGraphView
   buildAgentCausalGraphView   → AgentCausalGraphView
        │
        ▼ VizData  { kind: 'agent' | 'session' | 'interaction', data }
        │
        ▼ React Flow graph  (@coach/app)
```

View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by the graph renderer — no raw
`TraceNode[]` reaches the visualization layer.

### Fixture modes

`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under
`packages/pipeline/fixtures/`.

| Input shape                        | Mode                | Artifacts in `out/`                                            |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `<dir>/*.jsonl`                    | Native session      | `vizdata-<name>.json` per file                                 |
| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace.json`   | Single OTEL trace   | `vizdata-trace.json`                                           |
| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace-*.json` | Multi-trace session | per-trace + `vizdata-session.json` + `vizdata-agent.json`      |
| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | all of the above grouped by dir                                |

## Quick start

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm check             # typecheck + lint + format + test + knip (same as CI)
pnpm --filter @coach/app dev   # upload landing page at http://localhost:5173
```

## Development

| Command                         | What it does                                  |
| ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `pnpm check`                    | Full gate: typecheck, lint, format, test, knip |
| `pnpm lint:fix`                 | Auto-fix lint issues                           |
| `pnpm format`                   | Auto-format with Prettier                      |
| `pnpm --filter @coach/pipeline test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode              |
| `pnpm e2e <fixture>`            | Run pipeline on a fixture, write `out/`        |
| `pnpm enrich <fixture>`         | Enrich a single OTEL trace fixture             |

## Contributing workflow

- Branch off `main`; **never commit to `main` directly**.

# coach

Improve agent harnesses — accuracy, latency, cost, and detection of hallucinations and operational
errors — through **OpenTelemetry (OTEL) trace analysis**. OTEL keeps coach **harness-agnostic**.

Unlike tracing built for engineers to observe agents, coach aims to reflect findings back to the
**agent itself**, with the engineer monitoring that loop. Stage one targets the engineer until we
learn which problems are solvable.

See **[ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)** for the full picture: package layout, data flow, upload
seam, and Vercel deployment.

## Pipeline

```
*.jsonl / logs.json + trace*.json
        │
        ▼ ETL  (@coach/pipeline)
   nativeSessionToTrace / enrichTrace → transformTrace → addSessionNode
        │
        ▼ Aggregation
   aggregateSession  ·  groupSessionsByAgent → aggregateAgent
        │
        ▼ View model
   buildCausalGraphView        → CausalGraphView
   buildSessionCausalGraphView → SessionCausalGraphView
   buildAgentCausalGraphView   → AgentCausalGraphView
        │
        ▼ VizData  { kind: 'agent' | 'session' | 'interaction', data }
        │
        ▼ React Flow graph  (@coach/app)
```

View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by the graph renderer — no raw
`TraceNode[]` reaches the visualization layer.

### Fixture modes

`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under
`packages/pipeline/fixtures/`.

| Input shape                        | Mode                | Artifacts in `out/`                                            |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `<dir>/*.jsonl`                    | Native session      | `vizdata-<name>.json` per file                                 |
| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace.json`   | Single OTEL trace   | `vizdata-trace.json`                                           |
| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace-*.json` | Multi-trace session | per-trace + `vizdata-session.json` + `vizdata-agent.json`      |
| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | all of the above grouped by dir                                |

## Quick start

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm check             # typecheck + lint + format + test + knip (same as CI)
pnpm --filter @coach/app dev   # upload landing page at http://localhost:5173
```

## Development

| Command                         | What it does                                  |
| ------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `pnpm check`                    | Full gate: typecheck, lint, format, test, knip |
| `pnpm lint:fix`                 | Auto-fix lint issues                           |
| `pnpm format`                   | Auto-format with Prettier                      |
| `pnpm --filter @coach/pipeline test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode              |
| `pnpm e2e <fixture>`            | Run pipeline on a fixture, write `out/`        |
| `pnpm enrich <fixture>`         | Enrich a single OTEL trace fixture             |

## Contributing workflow

- Branch off `main`; **never commit to `main` directly**.
- Open a PR — CI runs on open, on every push to the branch, and on pushes to `main`.

### Recommended one-time setup (requires a GitHub remote)

Make CI a required check so PRs can't merge red:

```bash
gh api -X PUT repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/main/protection \
  -F required_status_checks.strict=true \
  -F 'required_status_checks.contexts[]=check' \
  -F enforce_admins=true \
  -F required_pull_request_reviews.required_approving_review_count=1 \
  -F restrictions=
```

# coach

Improve agent harnesses — accuracy, latency, cost, and detection of hallucinations and operational
errors — through **OpenTelemetry (OTEL) trace analysis**. OTEL keeps coach **harness-agnostic**.

Unlike tracing built for engineers to observe agents, coach aims to reflect findings back to the
**agent itself**, with the engineer monitoring that loop. Stage one targets the engineer until we
learn which problems are solvable.

## Pipeline

```
trace.json / logs.json
        │
        ▼ ETL (per trace)
   enrichTrace → transformTrace → addSessionNode
        │
        ▼ Aggregation
   aggregateSession (per session)
   groupSessionsByAgent → aggregateAgent (per user_id)
        │
        ▼ View model (bottom-up)
   buildCausalGraphView        → CausalGraphView
   buildSessionCausalGraphView → SessionCausalGraphView
   buildAgentCausalGraphView   → AgentCausalGraphView
        │
        ▼ HTML
   buildCausalHtml / buildSessionCausalHtml / buildAgentCausalHtml
```

View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by HTML renderers — no raw `TraceNode[]` reaches the rendering layer.

### Fixture modes

`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under `src/fixtures/`.

| Input shape                        | Mode                | HTML outputs                                            |
| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `<dir>/trace.json` + `logs.json`   | Single trace        | `composition`, `causal`                                 |
| `<dir>/trace-*.json` + `logs.json` | Multi-trace session | above + `causal-session`, `causal-agent`                |
| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | per-session outputs + `causal-agent-{userId}` per agent |

In multi-session mode the `user_id` embedded in each trace determines the agent — one HTML is produced per unique `user_id` across all sessions.

## Quick start

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm check     # typecheck + lint + format + test (same as CI)
```

## Development

| Command           | What it does                             |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `pnpm check`      | Full gate: typecheck, lint, format, test |
| `pnpm lint:fix`   | Auto-fix lint issues                     |
| `pnpm format`     | Auto-format with Prettier                |
| `pnpm test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode                     |
| `pnpm build`      | Emit `dist/`                             |

Quality is enforced deterministically by committed hooks — a git `pre-commit` hook (Husky +
lint-staged), a Claude Code `PostToolUse` hook, and GitHub Actions CI. See `CLAUDE.md` for details.

## Contributing workflow

- Branch off `main`; **never commit to `main` directly**.
- Open a PR — CI runs on open, on every push to the branch, and on pushes to `main`.

### Recommended one-time setup (requires a GitHub remote)

Make CI a required check so PRs can't merge red:

```bash
gh api -X PUT repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/main/protection \
  -F required_status_checks.strict=true \
  -F 'required_status_checks.contexts[]=check' \
  -F enforce_admins=true \
  -F required_pull_request_reviews.required_approving_review_count=1 \
  -F restrictions=
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# coach

Coach improves agent harnesses — accuracy, latency, cost, hallucination and operational-error
detection — by analyzing **OpenTelemetry (OTEL) traces**. OTEL is used deliberately so coach stays
**harness-agnostic**.

The thesis: existing agent tracing is built for the _engineer_ to observe and improve the agent.
Coach instead aims to reflect findings back to the **agent itself**, with the engineer monitoring
that loop. How the loop is ultimately "closed" back to the agent is undecided — so **stage one
targets the engineer** until we learn which problems are actually solvable.

## Tech stack

- **Language:** TypeScript (ESM, strict)
- **Package manager:** pnpm (do not use npm/yarn)
- **Lint/format:** ESLint (flat config, `strict-type-checked`) + Prettier
- **Dead code:** Knip (unused files, exports, dependencies)
- **Tests:** Vitest
- **CI:** GitHub Actions

## Commands

```bash
pnpm install          # install dependencies
pnpm check            # typecheck + lint + format:check + test + knip (what CI runs)
pnpm typecheck        # tsc --noEmit
pnpm lint             # eslint, warnings = errors (--max-warnings=0)
pnpm lint:fix         # eslint --fix
pnpm format           # prettier --write .
pnpm format:check     # prettier --check .
pnpm test             # vitest run
pnpm test:watch       # vitest (watch)
pnpm knip             # check for unused files, exports, and dependencies
pnpm build            # emit dist/ via tsup
```

## Quality gates are enforced by hooks, not by trust

Linting/formatting/type/test rules are enforced **deterministically**, in three committed layers —
not as polite requests in this file:

1. **`.husky/pre-commit`** — runs `lint-staged` (ESLint `--fix` + Prettier on staged files),
   `pnpm typecheck`, and `pnpm knip` on **every commit, by every contributor**. This is the primary gate.
2. **`.claude/hooks/lint.sh`** and **`pnpm knip`** (wired via `.claude/settings.json` `PostToolUse`) —
   formats, lints, and checks for dead code immediately after Claude edits a file.
3. **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — re-runs the full `pnpm check` (including knip) as the backstop
   that cannot be bypassed locally.

Strictness: TypeScript runs with `strict` plus extra safety flags; ESLint treats **warnings as
errors** (`--max-warnings=0`). Don't weaken these to make code pass — fix the code.

## Workflow rules

- **Never commit directly to `main`.** Every change goes through a branch + PR.
- CI runs on PR open, every push to the PR branch, and every push to `main`.
- A PR is mergeable only when `pnpm check` passes (enable branch protection on `main` so this is
  required — see README).
- Keep tests **basic** for now: cover the normal path and obvious error cases, not exotic edge cases.

## Code style

Prefer named functions and descriptive variable names over inline comments that narrate steps.
A function named `groupLogsBySpan()` is better than a `// 2. Group logs by span_id` comment
before an anonymous block. Comments are for non-obvious WHY, not for labelling WHAT.

## Layout

```
src/
  index.ts            # public exports
  etl/                # enrich + transform pipeline (+ co-located *.test.ts)
  graph/              # mermaid graph generation
.claude/
  settings.json       # committed Claude Code config (PostToolUse lint + knip hooks)
  hooks/lint.sh       # per-file lint hook script
.husky/pre-commit     # git pre-commit gate
.github/workflows/    # CI
```

lines: that loop. How the loop is ultimately "closed" back to the agent is undecided — so **stage one
targets the engineer** until we learn which problems are actually solvable.
+**`ARCHITECTURE.md` is a living document** — update it in the same change whenever package
+layout, module boundaries, or data flow change. Consult it before architectural tasks.

attachment.snippet:
1	> **Living document** — update this file in the same change whenever package layout,
2	> module boundaries, or data flow change. Keep it honest on structural changes; consult
3	> it before architectural tasks.
4	
5	# Coach — Architecture
6	
7	## Overview
8	
9	Coach processes agent execution traces and renders them as an interactive causal graph.
10	The core thesis: harness-agnostic OTEL traces feed a pure data pipeline whose output can
11	be reflected back to the agent (or its engineer) for improvement.
12	
13	The system is split into two packages plus a Node CLI layer:
14	
15	```
16	┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
17	│  @coach/app  (packages/app)                             │
18	│  React SPA · Upload UI · Graph renderer                 │
19	│                                                         │
20	│  upload/UploadPage.tsx ──► data-source.ts ──► viz/App   │
21	│                               │                         │
22	│           ┌───────────────────┘                         │
23	│           ▼                                             │
24	│  @coach/pipeline (packages/pipeline)                    │
25	│  Pure data processing · No node:* imports               │
26	│  ETL → Aggregate → View model → VizData                 │
27	└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
28	
29	scripts/          Node CLI — reads from disk, writes JSON artifacts
30	```
31	
32	## Package layout
33	
34	| Package / dir       | Purpose                                                                                                                  |
35	| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
36	| `packages/pipeline` | Pure data pipeline: ETL, aggregation, view-model, orchestration. Zero `node:*` imports — runs in browser and Node alike. |
37	| `packages/app`      | React SPA: upload landing page, graph visualization, data-source seam.                                                   |
38	| `scripts/`          | Node CLI over the same pipeline. Reads fixture files from disk, writes `out/*.json` artifacts.                           |
39	
40	## Data flow
41	
42	```
43	Input files
44	  *.jsonl                 native Claude Code session logs
45	  logs.json + trace*.json OTEL Tempo traces + structured log entries
46	        │
47	        ▼  packages/pipeline/src/etl/
48	   nativeSessionToTrace()    (*.jsonl → TempoTrace)
49	   enrichTrace()             (TempoTrace + LogEntry[] → enriched TempoTrace)
50	   transformTrace()          (TempoTrace → TraceNode[])
51	   addSessionNode()          (synthesise session node)
52	        │
53	        ▼  packages/pipeline/src/etl/aggregate.ts
54	   aggregateSession()        (merge multi-trace → session forest)
55	   aggregateAgent()          (add agent root node)
56	        │
57	        ▼  packages/pipeline/src/graph/view-model.ts
58	   buildCausalGraphView()        → CausalGraphView
59	   buildSessionCausalGraphView() → SessionCausalGraphView
60	   buildAgentCausalGraphView()   → AgentCausalGraphView
61	        │
62	        ▼  VizData  { kind: 'agent'|'session'|'interaction', data }
63	        │
64	        ▼  packages/app/src/viz/App.tsx  (React Flow graph renderer)
65	```
66	
67	View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by the renderer — no raw `TraceNode[]`
68	reaches the visualization layer.
69	
70	## Upload flow and the data-source seam
71	
72	```
73	Browser
74	  UploadPage.tsx
75	    └── File.text() × N → UploadedFile[]
76	          └── data-source.ts :: processUploads(files)
77	                └── @coach/pipeline :: buildVizResults(files)   ← CURRENT
78	                        └── ETL + aggregate + view model
79	                              └── VizResult[]
80	                                    └── App.tsx renders graph
81	```
82	
83	**`packages/app/src/data-source.ts` is the single swap point** for moving processing to a
84	backend. Replace its body with a `fetch('/api/process', ...)` call and nothing else in the
85	app changes — the visualization layer depends only on `VizResult` / `VizData`.
86	
87	## Fixture modes
88	
89	`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under
90	`packages/pipeline/fixtures/`.
91	
92	| Input shape                        | Mode                | Artifacts produced                                        |
93	| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
94	| `<dir>/*.jsonl`                    | Native session      | `vizdata-<name>.json` per file                            |
95	| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace.json`   | Single OTEL trace   | `vizdata-trace.json`                                      |
96	| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace-*.json` | Multi-trace session | per-trace + `vizdata-session.json` + `vizdata-agent.json` |
97	| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | all of the above, grouped by dir                          |
98	
99	Multi-session-by-`user_id` is supported by the CLI (directory walking). Flat browser
100	upload of multi-session data is a future item; consider `<input webkitdirectory>`.
101	
102	## Deploying to Vercel (static SPA)
103	
104	The app builds to static assets — no serverless functions.
105	
106	```
107	Root directory:  packages/app
108	Build command:   pnpm --filter @coach/app build
109	Output dir:      packages/app/dist
110	Install command: pnpm install (at repo root)
111	```
112	
113	See `vercel.json` for the committed configuration.

34	View models are built bottom-up and consumed only by the graph renderer — no raw
35	`TraceNode[]` reaches the visualization layer.
36	
37	### Fixture modes
38	
39	`pnpm e2e` accepts a path (relative to cwd) or a fixture name under
40	`packages/pipeline/fixtures/`.
41	
42	| Input shape                        | Mode                | Artifacts in `out/`                                       |
43	| ---------------------------------- | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
44	| `<dir>/*.jsonl`                    | Native session      | `vizdata-<name>.json` per file                            |
45	| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace.json`   | Single OTEL trace   | `vizdata-trace.json`                                      |
46	| `<dir>/logs.json` + `trace-*.json` | Multi-trace session | per-trace + `vizdata-session.json` + `vizdata-agent.json` |
47	| `<dir>/` containing session dirs   | Multi-session       | all of the above grouped by dir                           |
48	
49	## Quick start
50	
51	```bash
52	pnpm install
53	pnpm check             # typecheck + lint + format + test + knip (same as CI)
54	pnpm --filter @coach/app dev   # upload landing page at http://localhost:5173
55	```
56	
57	## Development
58	
59	| Command                                    | What it does                                   |
60	| ------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------- |
61	| `pnpm check`                               | Full gate: typecheck, lint, format, test, knip |
62	| `pnpm lint:fix`                            | Auto-fix lint issues                           |
63	| `pnpm format`                              | Auto-format with Prettier                      |
64	| `pnpm --filter @coach/pipeline test:watch` | Vitest in watch mode                           |
65	| `pnpm e2e <fixture>`                       | Run pipeline on a fixture, write `out/`        |
66	| `pnpm enrich <fixture>`                    | Enrich a single OTEL trace fixture             |
67	
68	## Contributing workflow
69	
70	- Branch off `main`; **never commit to `main` directly**.
71	- Open a PR — CI runs on open, on every push to the branch, and on pushes to `main`.
72	
73	### Recommended one-time setup (requires a GitHub remote)
74

name: CI

on:
  # Runs when a PR is opened and on every push to that PR's branch

  push:
    branches: [main]

# Cancel superseded runs on the same ref to save CI minutes.

  group: ci-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

          cache: pnpm

name: CI

on:
  # Runs when a PR is opened and on every push to that PR's branch

  pull_request:
  # Runs whenever main changes.
  push:

# Cancel superseded runs on the same ref to save CI minutes.
concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4

      - name: Setup Node
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 24
          cache: pnpm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile

      - name: Typecheck
        run: pnpm typecheck

      - name: Lint
        run: pnpm lint

      - name: Format check
        run: pnpm format:check

      - name: Test
        run: pnpm test

      - name: Knip
        run: pnpm knip

git -C /Users/ofek/projects/coach commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Restructure into pnpm monorepo: @coach/pipeline + @coach/app SPA

- packages/pipeline: pure ETL + view model (zero node:* imports, browser-safe)
  — replaces src/etl/ + src/graph/; adds orchestrate.ts (buildVizResults)
  — replaces Buffer/createHash with browser-compatible atob/btoa + FNV-1a hash
  — exports VizData + all view types as single source of truth
- packages/app: React SPA with upload landing page + React Flow graph
  — replaces viz/ (drops vite-plugin-singlefile, normal Vite build)
  — data-source.ts seam: swap one function to move processing to a backend
  — multi-result selector for multi-trace sessions
- scripts/: rewired to import from @coach/pipeline (no duplicated orchestration)
- pnpm workspace: root + packages/*; pnpm check runs across all packages
- ESLint, Knip, Husky, CI all updated for monorepo layout
- ARCHITECTURE.md: living doc covering package layout, data flow, upload seam, Vercel
- vercel.json: static deploy config (no serverless)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

gh pr create \
  --repo "$(git -C /Users/ofek/projects/coach remote get-url origin | sed 's|.*github.com[:/]||;s|\.git$||')" \
  --title "Restructure into pnpm monorepo: @coach/pipeline + @coach/app static SPA" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- **`@coach/pipeline`** (`packages/pipeline`) — pure ETL + view-model package with zero `node:*` imports; runs in both browser and Node.js. Exports `VizData` and all view types as the single source of truth. New `orchestrate.ts` (`buildVizResults`) encapsulates all mode-selection logic formerly duplicated in `scripts/e2e.ts`.
- **`@coach/app`** (`packages/app`) — React SPA with an upload landing page (drag-drop + file picker) and the React Flow graph renderer. Drops `vite-plugin-singlefile`; builds to normal static assets deployable on Vercel. `data-source.ts` is the single swap point to move processing to an HTTP backend later.
- **`scripts/`** — rewired to import from `@coach/pipeline`; no duplicated orchestration logic; emits `vizdata-*.json` artifacts.
- **Quality gates**: `pnpm check` (typecheck + lint + format + test + knip) passes across all three packages. 34 tests passing.
- **Vercel**: `vercel.json` committed; set Vercel project root to repo root, it will use the configured build/output settings.
- **`ARCHITECTURE.md`**: new living doc covering package layout, data flow, upload seam, and Vercel deployment; referenced from `README.md` and `CLAUDE.md`.

## Test plan

- [ ] `pnpm install && pnpm check` at repo root passes (typecheck, lint, format, 34 tests, knip)
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @coach/app dev` starts; landing page shows Upload button
- [ ] Upload `packages/pipeline/fixtures/native-claude/fetch-website/session.jsonl` → graph renders
- [ ] Upload `packages/pipeline/fixtures/otel/fetch-website/logs.json` + `trace.json` → graph renders

- [ ] `pnpm e2e fetch-website` writes `out/fetch-website/vizdata-*.json`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

content.content: https://github.com/ofekkir/coach/pull/14

content.text:
PR opened at https://github.com/ofekkir/coach/pull/14.

Here's what was built:

**`@coach/pipeline` (`packages/pipeline`)**
- All ETL, aggregation, and view-model code — zero `node:*` / `fs` imports, runs identically in browser and Node
- `Buffer`/`createHash` replaced with `atob`/`btoa` + a pure-JS FNV-1a hash
- New `orchestrate.ts` exports `buildVizResults(files: UploadedFile[]): VizResult[]` — the single orchestration entry point, replacing the logic that was duplicated in `scripts/e2e.ts`
- `VizData` and all graph view types exported once here; `viz/src/types.ts` deleted

**`@coach/app` (`packages/app`)**
- Landing page with drag-drop + file picker (`UploadPage.tsx`)
- `data-source.ts` — the documented seam: one function swap moves processing to an HTTP backend
- Multi-result selector bar (shown when a multi-trace session upload produces several views)
- `App.tsx` accepts `data`/`title` props instead of reading `window.__TRACE_DATA__`; drops `vite-plugin-singlefile`
- Vercel config in `vercel.json`

**Scripts** — rewired to `@coach/pipeline` with no duplicated mode logic; emit `vizdata-*.json` artifacts

**`ARCHITECTURE.md`** — living doc covering layout, data flow, upload seam, and Vercel deploy settings

content: PR #14 is open with the full pnpm monorepo restructure — `pnpm check` passes (typecheck, lint, 34 tests, knip). Next step is to verify the app works in the browser by running `pnpm --filter @coach/app dev` and uploading the fixture files. (disable recaps in /config)
